Title: Successful Login with Correct Credentials
Preconditions: User has an existing account with valid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User enters the username "john_doe".
2. User enters the password "securepassword123".
3. User clicks on the login button.
Expected Result: User is successfully authenticated and redirected to the home page.

Title: Failed Login with Incorrect Username
Preconditions: User does not have an account with the provided username.
Test Steps:
1. User enters the username "non_existent_user".
2. User enters the password "securepassword123".
3. User clicks on the login button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating unauthorized access.

Title: Failed Login with Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User has an existing account but enters an incorrect password.
Test Steps:
1. User enters the username "john_doe".
2. User enters the password "wrongpassword".
3. User clicks on the login button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating unauthorized access.

Title: Successful Registration with Valid Email and Password
Preconditions: User does not have an existing account.
Test Steps:
1. User enters the email "jane.doe@example.com".
2. User enters the password "securepassword123".
3. User clicks on the signup button.
Expected Result: User is successfully registered and redirected to the login page.

Title: Failed Registration with Invalid Email Format
Preconditions: User does not have an existing account.
Test Steps:
1. User enters the email "janedoeexample.com".
2. User enters the password "securepassword123".
3. User clicks on the signup button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating invalid email format.

Title: Failed Registration with Weak Password
Preconditions: User does not have an existing account.
Test Steps:
1. User enters the email "jane.doe@example.com".
2. User enters the password "123".
3. User clicks on the signup button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating weak password.

Title: Successful Profile Update with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an existing profile.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile page.
2. User updates the bio to "Software Developer".
3. User updates the location to "New York".
4. User clicks on the save button.
Expected Result: User's profile is successfully updated and the changes are reflected on the profile page.

Title: Failed Profile Update with Empty Bio Field
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an existing profile.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile page.
2. User leaves the bio field empty.
3. User updates the location to "New York".
4. User clicks on the save button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the bio field cannot be empty.

Title: Failed Profile Update with Invalid Location Format
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an existing profile.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile page.
2. User updates the bio to "Software Developer".
3. User updates the location to "12345".
4. User clicks on the save button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the location field must be a valid city name.

Title: Successful Exit with Reason Provided
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "Leaving for the day".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User is successfully exited from the application and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Failed Exit with Empty Exit Reason
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User leaves the exit reason field empty.
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the exit reason field cannot be empty.

Title: Successful Exit without Providing Exit Reason
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User clicks on the exit button without entering an exit reason.
Expected Result: User is successfully exited from the application and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Exit with Special Characters in Exit Reason
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "!@#$%^&*()".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User is successfully exited from the application and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Exit with Long Exit Reason
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Data: - ExitReason: "This is a very long exit reason that exceeds the maximum allowed length."
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "This is a very long exit reason that exceeds the maximum allowed length.".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the exit reason is too long.

Title: Exit with Short Exit Reason
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "X".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User is successfully exited from the application and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Exit with Numeric Exit Reason

Test Steps:

2. User enters the exit reason "1234567890".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User is successfully exited from the application and receives a confirmation message.

3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User is successfully exited from the application and receives a confirmation message.

Title: Exit with Special Characters and Spaces in Exit Reason
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "!@#$%^ &*()".

Title: Exit with Mixed Case Exit Reason

2. User enters the exit reason "Leaving fOr tHe DaY".

Title: Exit with Empty Exit Reason and No Confirmation Message
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.

2. User clicks on the exit button without entering an exit reason.
Expected Result: User is successfully exited from the application without receiving a confirmation message.

Title: Exit with Exit Reason Containing HTML Tags
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "<html><body>Leaving for the day</body></html>".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the exit reason contains invalid characters.

Title: Exit with Exit Reason Containing SQL Injection
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "'; DROP TABLE users;".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the exit reason contains invalid characters.

Title: Exit with Exit Reason Containing JavaScript Code
Preconditions: User is logged in and wants to exit the application.
Test Steps:
1. User decides to exit the application.
2. User enters the exit reason "<script>alert('XSS');</script>".
3. User clicks on the exit button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating that the exit reason contains invalid characters.
